title: Root test command fails on deleted ProDashboard path
currentEvidence: `npm run test` exits 1 with ENOENT for apps/control-plane/src/components/pro/ProDashboard.tsx from packages/validation/src/tests/proDashboardTruthState.test.ts.
enterpriseRequirement: Root test suite must pass and must test canonical product paths.
gap: Current root tests cannot be used as launch gate because they fail before exercising current VibeDashboard/AppShell path.
proofNeeded: Updated root test suite passes against canonical AppShell/VibeDashboard routes and fails on regressions.
recommendedFix: Update stale test expectations in a later validator PR and require `npm run test` in CI.
riskIfIgnored: A broken release gate can hide real regressions and blocks any credible launch.

title: Full validation is red with 18 failing validators
currentEvidence: `npm run validate:all` exits 1; failed validators include Validate-Botomatic-UIReadiness, Validate-Botomatic-UIControlPlaneIntegration, Validate-Botomatic-LiveUIBuilderDataStateApiWiringReadiness, Validate-Botomatic-LiveUIBuilderReliabilityRepairReadiness, Validate-Botomatic-LiveUIBuilderUXPolishReadiness, Validate-Botomatic-LiveUIBuilderExportDeployReadiness, Validate-Botomatic-LiveUIBuilderPlatformBuilderReadiness, Validate-Botomatic-DashboardRouteIntegrityReadiness, Validate-Botomatic-LiveUIBuilderInteractionReadiness, Validate-Botomatic-LiveUIBuilderVisualReadiness, Validate-Botomatic-LiveUIBuilderInteractionUXReadiness, Validate-Botomatic-LiveUIBuilderSourceSyncReadiness, ....
enterpriseRequirement: Enterprise launch requires all current validators to pass or be intentionally replaced by stricter fail-closed gates.
gap: Validation registry is not green and includes stale or incomplete UI/live-builder checks.
proofNeeded: `npm run validate:all` passes after stale validators are corrected and missing proofs added.
recommendedFix: Separate validator remediation PR: update stale paths, remove permissive checks, add missing proof validators.
riskIfIgnored: Launch claims can be contradicted by failing gates and stale checks.

title: No proven tenant/project ownership isolation
currentEvidence: Supabase schema `orchestrator_projects` uses `project_id` as primary key and no owner/tenant column or RLS policy was found in supabase/migrations/001_init.sql; many status/API routes fetch by projectId only.
enterpriseRequirement: Every project, API route, job, artifact, log, and evidence item must be scoped to authenticated tenant/user with RLS or equivalent authorization checks.
gap: Project access appears keyed primarily by project ID, with no current proof that one authenticated user cannot access another project.
proofNeeded: Negative-path tenant isolation test covering UI/API/job/evidence access across two users and durable database RLS/policies.
recommendedFix: Add tenant/owner fields, RLS/policies, route authorization checks, and isolation proof.
riskIfIgnored: Cross-customer data exposure, enterprise deal failure, and severe compliance/security incident.

title: Development auth-disabled/anonymous path exists and must be impossible in production
currentEvidence: config.ts returns auth disabled in non-commercial mode; server_app.ts maps unauthenticated users to anonymous/operator and getRequestActor catches auth errors as anonymous.
enterpriseRequirement: Production must fail closed and never allow production-looking deployments to accept anonymous operator actions.
gap: There is a safe commercial guard, but deployment proof must show production cannot run with RUNTIME_MODE unset/development or with auth disabled while publicly reachable.
proofNeeded: Production boot test and deployment smoke proving public env refuses missing auth and cannot start with disabled auth.
recommendedFix: Add explicit environment guardrails and CI/deploy checks that fail production if auth is disabled or runtime mode is development.
riskIfIgnored: Anonymous access to project operations or evidence in a public deployment.

title: Many project routes lack visible requireRole wrapper
currentEvidence: Express route map shows several project routes such as intake/status/state/runtime/operator/send/compile/plan with no `requireRole` wrapper visible; some admin/reviewer routes are protected.
enterpriseRequirement: All customer/project routes need explicit authentication and authorization appropriate to action sensitivity.
gap: Route-level auth is inconsistent: high-sensitivity flows rely on getVerifiedAuth/getRequestActor internally or no wrapper instead of uniform route middleware.
proofNeeded: Route authorization matrix plus negative-path tests for every route.
recommendedFix: Add explicit auth middleware to all project routes and prove denied access without valid identity/role.
riskIfIgnored: Unauthorized project creation, compilation, status reads, or operator commands.

title: No current full secret scan across source, history, evidence, logs, and UI
currentEvidence: `validate:all` reports a secret credential management validator pass, but this audit only performed a pattern spot-check and did not prove git history/log/evidence/UI redaction.
enterpriseRequirement: Enterprise launch requires current full-repo plus history plus generated evidence/log/UI secret scan with machine-readable proof.
gap: Secret-scan proof is incomplete for the full enterprise boundary requested here.
proofNeeded: `proof:no-secrets` covering source, git history, release evidence, receipts, logs, generated apps, UI responses, and redaction assertions.
recommendedFix: Run/add full proof later; store clean artifact and block release on failures.
riskIfIgnored: Credential leakage to customers, repository, artifacts, or logs.

title: No proven real end-to-end orchestration path under durable mode
currentEvidence: Build passes and validators claim pieces, but root tests fail and no current proof exercised intake -> compile -> plan -> queue -> worker -> executor -> materialization -> preview -> validation in durable mode.
enterpriseRequirement: Core build/orchestration must reliably work end-to-end with durable persistence and real executor/preview/evidence.
gap: Current proof is fragmented and mixed with local/mock evidence; enterprise-grade durable E2E proof is missing.
proofNeeded: `proof:orchestration-core` that runs the full durable path, restarts services, and verifies final app/materialization/evidence.
recommendedFix: Create full integration proof in a later PR using isolated test project and durable backend.
riskIfIgnored: Customers may pay for a builder that cannot complete real builds reliably.

title: Durable/Supabase outage can silently downgrade to memory/development mode
currentEvidence: bootstrap.ts changes PROJECT_REPOSITORY_MODE to memory and RUNTIME_MODE to development when Supabase probe fails; queue can fall back to memory.
enterpriseRequirement: Production deployments must fail closed or enter explicit maintenance when durable persistence/queue is unavailable.
gap: Fallback protects local dev but is unsafe if reachable production ever downgrades to ephemeral memory.
proofNeeded: Production-mode startup test proving Supabase outage aborts or fails readiness rather than accepting traffic.
recommendedFix: Gate fallback to development only and add production readiness failure.
riskIfIgnored: Data loss, split-brain queues, lost jobs, and false success during outage.

title: Dashboard route validator expects deleted/replaced `/vibe` path
currentEvidence: `validate:all` fails DashboardRouteIntegrityReadiness: missing apps/control-plane/src/app/projects/[projectId]/vibe/page.tsx while current build lists /projects/[projectId].
enterpriseRequirement: Validators must match canonical current product paths and fail on real regressions.
gap: A stale validator makes route integrity signal unreliable.
proofNeeded: Updated validator verifies `/projects/[projectId]` renders VibeDashboard/AppShell and old route decisions are explicit.
recommendedFix: Update validator later; do not re-add old route just to satisfy stale gate.
riskIfIgnored: Teams may reintroduce stale routes or ignore real route regressions.

title: Core legal/commercial documents are missing
currentEvidence: Missing root files: LICENSE, NOTICE, TERMS.md, PRIVACY.md, SECURITY.md, DPA.md, SUBPROCESSORS.md, ACCEPTABLE_USE.md, SLA.md, BILLING.md, SUPPORT.md, CHANGELOG.md.
enterpriseRequirement: Enterprise commercial launch requires license/NOTICE, Terms, Privacy, Security Policy, DPA/subprocessors, Acceptable Use, SLA/support/billing/refund terms as applicable.
gap: Repo contains claim-boundary docs but not customer-ready legal/commercial docs.
proofNeeded: Legal document checklist reviewed by counsel and linked in product/docs.
recommendedFix: Add legal/commercial documents in a separate non-code PR after counsel review.
riskIfIgnored: Cannot sell enterprise/commercial SaaS credibly; legal and procurement blockers.

title: Billing, plan, usage, and entitlement enforcement are absent or unproven
currentEvidence: Search found generated marketing/pricing artifacts and claim docs, but no live Stripe/billing/entitlement route model for Botomatic control plane.
enterpriseRequirement: Paid SaaS must enforce plan, usage limits, billing status, cancellation/refunds, and entitlements server-side.
gap: If Botomatic is paid SaaS, monetization controls are not proven in live product.
proofNeeded: Billing/entitlement integration tests plus product routes and server-side enforcement proof.
recommendedFix: Implement or explicitly scope out paid SaaS before launch; add plan enforcement proof.
riskIfIgnored: Unpaid use, overuse, support disputes, and procurement failure.

title: Duplicate enqueue protection is incomplete/unknown
currentEvidence: Queue enqueue creates random job IDs for same packet; migration primary key is job_id, not unique project_id+packet_id queued/running; code catches requeue errors but duplicate prevention is not proven.
enterpriseRequirement: Each packet should have idempotent enqueue semantics and prevent duplicate concurrent execution.
gap: Same packet may be enqueued multiple times unless a deeper constraint exists outside observed schema.
proofNeeded: Concurrent enqueue/idempotency test and DB uniqueness constraint proof.
recommendedFix: Add idempotency keys/unique index and test duplicate dispatch/replay.
riskIfIgnored: Duplicate commits, conflicting PRs, double charges, inconsistent packet status.

title: Dead-letter/retry/stuck-job lifecycle is not enterprise-proven
currentEvidence: Supabase `claim_job` reclaims expired running jobs and finalizeJob records status; no dead-letter table/max retry/backoff/stuck alert proof was found in core queue schema.
enterpriseRequirement: Queues need bounded retries, dead-letter state, stuck job detection, operator remediation, and alerts.
gap: Current queue can reclaim expired jobs but lacks proven retry budgets/dead-letter operational path.
proofNeeded: Queue failure-mode test suite: worker crash, executor failure, stuck lease, retry exhaustion, dead-letter, alert.
recommendedFix: Add retry_count/dead_letter fields or equivalent and operational proof.
riskIfIgnored: Jobs can loop, disappear, or block builds without actionable support signal.

title: Restart/resume behavior not proven across API, worker, runner, and UI
currentEvidence: Project resume route exists and worker leases exist, but no current end-to-end restart proof was run in this audit.
enterpriseRequirement: Enterprise builder must resume after process restart without losing project, queue, execution, preview, or evidence state.
gap: Restart/resume is implemented in pieces but lacks launch-grade proof.
proofNeeded: Durable restart proof that kills/restarts services mid-build and verifies no duplicate/lost work.
recommendedFix: Add restart/resume integration test and evidence artifact.
riskIfIgnored: Users lose builds or see inconsistent state after deploys/restarts.

title: Vibe Builder commercial usefulness is not proven
currentEvidence: Current `/projects/[projectId]` renders VibeDashboard and uses orchestration hooks; validation failures include LiveUIBuilderDataStateApiWiring, UXPolish, ExportDeploy, PlatformBuilder, SourceSync, FullProjectGeneration, DesignSystem.
enterpriseRequirement: Commercial Vibe Builder must edit real projects, sync source, generate full apps, export/deploy, and present polished UX.
gap: Core UI exists but multiple commercial live-builder capabilities are incomplete or failing validation.
proofNeeded: End-to-end Vibe Builder proof editing a real project, syncing source, previewing, validating, exporting/deploying.
recommendedFix: Implement missing live-builder waves and prove real-data path.
riskIfIgnored: Product may look polished but fail to deliver commercially useful build/edit workflow.

title: Live UI fake/demo/seed leakage not fully proven absent
currentEvidence: VibeDashboard has hardcoded suggestion/action chips; proSeedData.ts exists; no `proof:no-demo-live-ui` was run.
enterpriseRequirement: Live UI must use real data or explicitly gated demo mode, with no fake/demo leakage in customer flows.
gap: Hardcoded UI affordances may be fine, but live-data boundary is unproven.
proofNeeded: No-demo-live-ui validator scanning rendered pages/API responses and verifying demo mode gates.
recommendedFix: Add explicit demo-mode guards and rendered-output proof.
riskIfIgnored: Customers may see fake readiness, fake metrics, or seed artifacts.

title: Accessibility/responsiveness proof missing
currentEvidence: Next build passes but no a11y/responsive test output was run; visual tests exist but root validation is red.
enterpriseRequirement: Enterprise UI needs keyboard navigation, screen-reader labels, contrast, responsive layouts, and automated checks.
gap: No current accessibility/responsive proof for canonical AppShell/VibeDashboard routes.
proofNeeded: Playwright/aXe/responsive screenshot proof for all customer-facing routes.
recommendedFix: Add accessibility and responsive gates.
riskIfIgnored: Poor usability, procurement blockers, and legal/accessibility risk.

title: No rate limiting or abuse protection proof
currentEvidence: Search did not identify rate-limit middleware on operator/intake/upload routes; upload limits exist for file size but not request abuse.
enterpriseRequirement: Public APIs need rate limiting, quotas, abuse detection, and brute-force protection.
gap: High-cost AI/build routes may be callable repeatedly without proven throttling.
proofNeeded: Rate-limit tests for auth failures, operator/send, intake/upload, and executor dispatch.
recommendedFix: Add middleware and per-tenant usage controls.
riskIfIgnored: Cost blowups, denial of service, and abuse of AI/executor resources.

title: CSRF/browser-origin protection is not proven
currentEvidence: CORS allowlist exists, but no CSRF token/same-site proof was found for browser-initiated mutating requests.
enterpriseRequirement: Browser-accessible mutating routes need CSRF/session strategy or proof that bearer-only API is not cookie-authenticated.
gap: CSRF applicability is unknown because auth/session model is not fully documented/proven.
proofNeeded: Security proof documenting auth transport and CSRF negative tests where applicable.
recommendedFix: Document bearer-only model or add CSRF protection for cookie/session auth.
riskIfIgnored: Cross-site actions if cookies/session auth are introduced or already used.

title: Service-role key boundary and RLS posture unproven
currentEvidence: Durable repository uses SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY; migration has no RLS policies visible.
enterpriseRequirement: Service-role keys must remain server-only; customer access must use RLS or server authorization.
gap: No proof that service-role key cannot leak to frontend/logs and no DB-level tenant isolation policy.
proofNeeded: Secret-boundary test plus Supabase RLS/policy proof.
recommendedFix: Add RLS/tenant policies and service key redaction tests.
riskIfIgnored: Database-wide access if key leaks or route authorization fails.

title: Data retention/deletion/export lifecycle missing
currentEvidence: No customer data retention/deletion/export docs or routes were found; project table cascades jobs on delete but no product API/process is evident.
enterpriseRequirement: Enterprise product needs retention schedules, user export, deletion, legal hold, and auditability.
gap: Customer data lifecycle is not defined or proven.
proofNeeded: `proof:data-retention` covering create/export/delete/retention/evidence/log cleanup.
recommendedFix: Implement data lifecycle routes/docs and proof.
riskIfIgnored: Compliance failures and inability to satisfy customer deletion/export requests.

title: Backup/restore process is missing
currentEvidence: Supabase migration exists, but no backup/restore runbook/proof was found.
enterpriseRequirement: Enterprise operations require backup schedules, restore drills, RPO/RTO, and migration rollback.
gap: Durability does not include proven backups/restores.
proofNeeded: Backup/restore drill evidence with RPO/RTO and rollback validation.
recommendedFix: Add backup/restore runbook and periodic proof.
riskIfIgnored: Permanent customer data loss or extended outage.

title: Staging/production separation is not proven
currentEvidence: Vercel/Railway/Supabase configs exist, but no explicit staging/prod environment separation proof was found.
enterpriseRequirement: Release process must separate staging and production credentials, databases, URLs, smoke tests, and approvals.
gap: Deployment config exists but environment separation proof is missing.
proofNeeded: Staging/prod smoke evidence with distinct env manifests and blocked cross-env access.
recommendedFix: Define env matrix and add smoke gates for both staging and production.
riskIfIgnored: Accidental prod writes from staging or untested deployment drift.

title: Production smoke and rollback proof incomplete
currentEvidence: Deployment/rollback routes and docs exist; no current production smoke command was run and root validate is red.
enterpriseRequirement: Every release needs staging smoke, production smoke, rollback drill, and recorded artifact.
gap: Rollback exists in code but release-grade smoke/rollback proof is incomplete.
proofNeeded: `proof:deployment-smoke` for staging/prod plus rollback drill evidence.
recommendedFix: Add repeatable smoke/rollback scripts and CI/release gates.
riskIfIgnored: Bad deploys may reach customers with no validated rollback path.

title: Stage-level observability is partial
currentEvidence: Metrics/errors/queue endpoints and alert webhook references exist, but no current proof maps every orchestration stage to logs/metrics/traces/alerts.
enterpriseRequirement: Enterprise ops needs health, readiness, logs, metrics, diagnostics, alerts, and run correlation per stage.
gap: Observability exists but not proven complete across intake/compile/plan/queue/worker/executor/materialization/validation.
proofNeeded: Stage observability proof with correlation IDs and alert negative-path tests.
recommendedFix: Instrument every stage and expand observability proof.
riskIfIgnored: Support cannot diagnose customer failures quickly.

title: Enterprise admin/operator/support docs missing or incomplete
currentEvidence: README/DEPLOY/local docs exist, but no dedicated enterprise admin, support, incident, API reference, data lifecycle, or compliance onboarding set was found.
enterpriseRequirement: Enterprise customers need accurate setup, admin, SSO/RBAC, API, support, troubleshooting, and incident docs.
gap: Docs are broad but not complete enough for enterprise procurement/onboarding.
proofNeeded: Docs review checklist and customer dry-run onboarding proof.
recommendedFix: Create enterprise docs pack and validate with fresh environment setup.
riskIfIgnored: Support load, failed onboarding, and sales/procurement friction.

title: Memory-mode worker parity with durable mode is unproven
currentEvidence: Bootstrap supports memory fallbacks; durable uses Supabase job functions while memory behavior is different/inferred.
enterpriseRequirement: Development and durable modes should have documented differences and parity tests for critical behavior.
gap: Mode divergence can hide bugs in local validation.
proofNeeded: Mode parity test matrix for memory and durable queue/repository.
recommendedFix: Add parity tests and mark non-parity behavior explicitly.
riskIfIgnored: Local passes may not predict production behavior.

title: Executor reliability and sandboxing proof incomplete
currentEvidence: ClaudeCodeExecutor/MockExecutor exist; bootstrap spawns claude runner when EXECUTOR=claude; no current executor sandbox/cost/timeouts proof was run.
enterpriseRequirement: Real executor must be sandboxed, time-limited, observable, and safe with customer inputs/secrets.
gap: Executor path is present but enterprise safety/reliability proof is incomplete.
proofNeeded: Executor integration proof with timeout, failure, secret redaction, sandbox boundaries, and cost controls.
recommendedFix: Add executor hardening tests and runtime limits.
riskIfIgnored: Runaway jobs, source leakage, high costs, or unsafe writes.

title: Commercial/pro cockpit route absent or intentionally replaced but not documented
currentEvidence: Current route tree has no /commercial, /pro, /cockpit, /advanced, or /vibe page; VibeDashboard appears canonical.
enterpriseRequirement: Enterprise product map must explicitly define whether cockpit/pro concepts are removed, merged, or replaced.
gap: Product IA decision is not documented as enterprise target.
proofNeeded: Canonical route/product IA decision document and route validator.
recommendedFix: Document canonical route map and adjust validators/docs.
riskIfIgnored: Sales/demo confusion and stale docs/tests.

title: Some validators pass despite current root gates failing
currentEvidence: validate:all includes PASS messages claiming final launch/commercial evidence while the command overall fails and root test fails.
enterpriseRequirement: Readiness validators must not emit launch-ready claims when required gates are red.
gap: Validator messaging is overconfident relative to global failure state.
proofNeeded: Meta-validator that blocks final readiness claims unless all required validators/tests pass.
recommendedFix: Make final validators aggregate global gate state fail-closed.
riskIfIgnored: Stakeholders may rely on misleading PASS messages.

title: No accepted enterprise-launch proof bundle
currentEvidence: There are many proof scripts and evidence docs, but no current single `proof:enterprise-launch` artifact was found/run that covers all categories with red/green status.
enterpriseRequirement: Enterprise launch needs one authoritative evidence bundle linking all proofs and command outputs.
gap: Proof artifacts are fragmented and some stale.
proofNeeded: `proof:enterprise-launch` generating signed/dated JSON+MD evidence bundle.
recommendedFix: Add unified proof bundle after underlying gaps close.
riskIfIgnored: Hard to audit release readiness; procurement and internal gates slow down.

title: CI gates do not include root `npm run test`, lint, or typecheck
currentEvidence: GitHub workflow runs build, test:universal, doctor, validate:all; root lint/typecheck scripts are missing and root test currently fails locally.
enterpriseRequirement: CI should run the exact release gate suite, including lint/typecheck and canonical tests.
gap: Current CI may omit known-failing root test and cannot run missing lint/typecheck.
proofNeeded: CI evidence showing build/test/validate/lint/typecheck all run and pass.
recommendedFix: Add/fix release scripts in later PR and make CI authoritative.
riskIfIgnored: Code quality/type regressions may bypass release gates.

title: Railway/Vercel deployment reproducibility is incomplete
currentEvidence: Procfile, nixpacks, and Vercel config exist; no locked staging/prod deploy manifest or reproducibility proof was found.
enterpriseRequirement: Deployments should be reproducible from clean checkout with pinned env, build, start, smoke, and rollback steps.
gap: Configs exist but reproducible deployment proof is incomplete.
proofNeeded: Clean-room staging/prod deployment proof with env manifest and smoke result.
recommendedFix: Create reproducible deployment runbook/scripts and smoke artifacts.
riskIfIgnored: Environment drift and failed launches.

title: AI vendor data handling and disclosure incomplete
currentEvidence: Docs mention Anthropic/OpenAI credentials and generated apps, but no customer-facing AI data handling/disclosure policy was found.
enterpriseRequirement: Enterprise customers need disclosure of AI vendor usage, prompt/output storage, retention, and opt-out/security posture.
gap: AI data processing boundary is not customer-ready.
proofNeeded: Privacy/DPA/AI disclosure docs plus tests proving prompt/output storage boundaries.
recommendedFix: Add AI data handling disclosures and storage controls.
riskIfIgnored: Procurement/legal failure and customer trust risk.

title: Dependency vulnerability gate not proven current
currentEvidence: No `npm audit` or dependency vulnerability command was requested/run; node_modules present and dependency surface includes Next/Express/multer/etc.
enterpriseRequirement: Enterprise release needs current dependency vulnerability policy and scan gate.
gap: Dependency risk is unknown for this audit.
proofNeeded: Dependency scan artifact with severity threshold and remediation/exception process.
recommendedFix: Add dependency scanning to CI/release gates.
riskIfIgnored: Known vulnerable dependency may ship.

title: Incident response and support workflow missing
currentEvidence: No SUPPORT.md/SLA/incident runbook root files found.
enterpriseRequirement: Enterprise operations require incident intake, severity levels, comms, escalation, postmortem, and support SLA.
gap: Operational response process is not defined in repo.
proofNeeded: Incident runbook and support workflow reviewed in tabletop exercise.
recommendedFix: Add support/incident docs and on-call/alert workflow.
riskIfIgnored: Slow incident response and enterprise contract blockers.

title: Historical receipts/generated artifacts create release-truth noise
currentEvidence: Repository contains receipts/** and release-evidence generated project artifacts; prior census marked many deprecated/unknown.
enterpriseRequirement: Enterprise repo should distinguish canonical evidence from archived historical artifacts.
gap: Old artifacts can be mistaken for current proof or leak stale claims.
proofNeeded: Evidence retention/archive policy and manifest marking current vs historical artifacts.
recommendedFix: Archive/label in cleanup PR after proof retention decision.
riskIfIgnored: Stale proof confusion, overclaims, or accidental disclosure.

title: Overclaim wording remains in docs/evidence
currentEvidence: Prior path census and scans found many files with final/commercial/production/100% wording; current validators are red.
enterpriseRequirement: No public/internal launch docs should overclaim beyond proof-backed status.
gap: Claim-boundary policy exists but content still requires cleanup against current gate failures.
proofNeeded: Overclaim scan proof with allowlist for examples and current evidence links.
recommendedFix: Run claim cleanup batch after canonical proof status is established.
riskIfIgnored: Misrepresentation to customers/investors and internal decision errors.

title: Root workspace layout does not include all source packages
currentEvidence: Root workspaces only include apps/control-plane and apps/orchestrator-api; scripts directly execute packages/* and apps/claude-runner.
enterpriseRequirement: Workspace/package layout should match all maintained packages for install/test/tooling consistency.
gap: Packages are source-controlled but not npm workspaces, creating tooling ambiguity.
proofNeeded: Package-layout decision and install/test proof for fresh clone.
recommendedFix: Either add workspaces or document intentional non-workspace package model.
riskIfIgnored: Fresh clone/tooling drift and dependency confusion.

currentEvidence: AppShell/VibeDashboard exist, but no current navigation e2e proof was run in this audit.
enterpriseRequirement: Every route and command navigation path should be covered by e2e tests.
gap: Navigation is likely present but proof is incomplete.
proofNeeded: Route/navigation Playwright test covering sidebar and command actions.
recommendedFix: Add nav coverage.
riskIfIgnored: Broken links or hidden routes may ship.

currentEvidence: UI components for ErrorCallout, EmptyState, LoadingSkeleton exist, but page-level coverage was not proven.
